name: Community Services and Employment Training, Inc. (Cset)
description_short: CSET is a California non-profit providing free financial counseling, employment training, housing assistance, and community development services to Visalia residents.
description_long:
Community Services and Employment Training, Inc. (CSET) is a non-profit organization based in Visalia, California, operating under the mission statement "Helping People, Changing Lives." The organization has established itself as a multi-service community resource center addressing interconnected social and economic challenges affecting low-income families and individuals in the region.

CSET offers a comprehensive portfolio of services spanning four primary domains: Family and Community Development (senior services, family resource centers, recycling, urban forestry, public works, and navigation center support); Housing (energy assistance, home weatherization, first-time homebuyer education, foreclosure counseling, and continuum of care); Youth and Young Adults (community corps, charter school operation, technology training, leadership development, and employment preparation); and Financial Strength and Employment (job placement, income tax preparation, business hub support, and welfare-to-work services). Free income tax preparation is prominently featured, indicating a commitment to direct financial assistance.

CSET distinguishes itself through its integrated approach to community support, operating youth programs including a charter school (John Muir Charter School) and employment corps alongside traditional counseling services. The organization addresses homelessness through navigation center operations and provides specialized services like home weatherization and urban forestry that extend beyond typical non-profit counseling models. This breadth suggests CSET functions as a comprehensive community anchor rather than a single-issue organization.

As a non-profit operating under the free-help category, CSET's primary limitation is geographic—services appear restricted to Visalia, California. The website provides limited detail on specific counselor certifications (such as HUD-approval or NFCC certification status), making it difficult to verify counselor qualifications independently. Consumers outside the Visalia area will need to seek comparable services locally.
pros: Free income tax preparation services directly reducing tax burden for low-income households
Integrated housing counseling including foreclosure prevention and first-time homebuyer education
Employment Connection and Jobs programs providing job placement beyond generic counseling
Energy Assistance and Home Weatherization programs reducing utility costs for eligible households
Youth employment programs (Youth@Work, Sequoia Community Corps) creating pipeline from training to jobs
Senior Services addressing aging population financial and community needs
Multi-sector approach addressing employment, housing, and family stability simultaneously
cons: Services geographically limited to Visalia, California area with no remote or online service indication
Website lacks specific information on counselor credentials, certifications, or HUD-approval status
No details provided on eligibility requirements, income limits, or service availability constraints
Limited transparency regarding wait times, service capacity, or how to access different service lines
No information on whether financial counseling services follow NFCC standards or certification
best_for: Visalia, California residents seeking free tax preparation and employment assistance
Low-income families needing integrated housing counseling, financial guidance, and job training
First-time homebuyers and renters at risk of foreclosure in the Tulare County area
Youth and young adults (ages 16-24) seeking employment training and career pathway development
diagnosis: CSET is best for Visalia-area residents needing free, comprehensive community support spanning employment, housing, tax preparation, and family services. The primary caveat is strict geographic limitation to Visalia, California—consumers outside this area will need to identify comparable local non-profit resources.
services: Employment Connection and job placement services
Youth employment training (Youth@Work, Sequoia Community Corps)
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
meta_description: CSET is a California non-profit providing free financial counseling, employment training, and housing assistance in Visalia.
